# Test server error handling and special cases

# Create a test server with various error scenarios
mkdir -p cmd/mcp-test-server
cat > cmd/mcp-test-server/main.go << 'EOF'
package main

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"os"

	"github.com/tmc/mcp"
)

type testTransport struct {
	io.ReadWriteCloser
	shouldError bool
}

func (t *testTransport) Close() error {
	if t.shouldError {
		return errors.New("test close error")
	}
	return nil
}

func main() {
	server := mcp.NewServer("error-test-server", "1.0.0")

	// Register a tool that might error
	tool := mcp.Tool{
		Name:        "error_tool",
		Description: "Tool that can return errors",
		InputSchema: []byte(`{"type":"object","properties":{"should_error":{"type":"boolean"}}}`),
	}

	server.RegisterTool(tool, func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
		var args map[string]interface{}
		if err := json.Unmarshal(req.Arguments, &args); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		
		if shouldError, ok := args["should_error"].(bool); ok && shouldError {
			return nil, errors.New("intentional error")
		}
		
		return &mcp.CallToolResult{
			Content: []any{
				mcp.TextContent{
					Type: "text",
					Text: "Success",
				},
			},
		}, nil
	})

	// Test various server configurations
	testCase := os.Getenv("TEST_CASE")
	
	switch testCase {
	case "close_error":
		// Test transport with close error
		transport := &testTransport{
			ReadWriteCloser: mcp.StdioTransport(os.Stdin, os.Stdout),
			shouldError:     true,
		}
		if err := server.Serve(context.Background(), transport); err != nil {
			fmt.Printf("Server ended with error (expected): %v\n", err)
		}
		
	default:
		// Normal operation
		transport := mcp.StdioTransport(os.Stdin, os.Stdout)
		if err := server.Serve(context.Background(), transport); err != nil {
			log.Fatal(err)
		}
	}
}
EOF

# Test 1: Normal operation with error tool
echo "Testing error handling in tools..."
mcptrace -- go run cmd/mcp-test-server/main.go <<'STDIN' > error1.out
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"error_tool","arguments":{"should_error":true}},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"error_tool","arguments":{"should_error":false}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"_debug/exit","params":{},"id":4}
STDIN

# Check error handling
grep -q '"error"' error1.out || echo "Error response not found"
grep -q 'intentional error' error1.out || echo "Error message not found"
grep -q '"Success"' error1.out || echo "Success response not found"

# Test 2: Transport close error
echo "Testing transport close error..."
TEST_CASE=close_error mcptrace -- go run cmd/mcp-test-server/main.go <<'STDIN' > error2.out 2>&1
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"_debug/exit","params":{},"id":2}
STDIN

grep -q 'test close error' error2.out || echo "Close error not handled properly"

echo "Error handling tests completed"
